---
title: "Architecture"
---

## Data flow

```
.ferx model file  ──► parser/model_parser.rs  ──► CompiledModel
NONMEM CSV        ──► io/datareader.rs         ──► Population
                                                        │
                                               api::fit()
                                                        │
                              ┌─────────────────────────┤
                              │                         │
                    outer_optimizer            inner_optimizer
                    (population θ, Ω, σ)     (per-subject EBE η)
                              │                         │
                         pk/  or  ode/           stats/likelihood
                    (predictions)              (NLL evaluation)
                              │
                         FitResult  ──► R via extendr FFI
```

The outer optimizer iterates over population parameters (theta, omega, sigma).
For each candidate parameter set it calls the inner optimizer once per subject
to find the empirical Bayes estimate (EBE) of that subject's random effects.
Predictions are routed to the analytical PK models in `pk/` or the RK45 ODE
solver in `ode/`, depending on whether the model file has an `[odes]` block.

## FFI boundary

ferx-r wraps ferx-core via [extendr](https://extendr.github.io/). The glue
layer in `src/rust/src/lib.rs` (ferx-r repo) exposes three `#[extendr]`
functions:

- `ferx_rust_fit` — calls `api::run_model_with_data`, returns a named R list
- `ferx_rust_simulate` — calls `api::simulate`, returns an R data frame
- `ferx_rust_predict` — calls `api::predict`, returns an R data frame

Conversion helpers:

- `fit_result_to_list()` — unpacks `FitResult` into an R list (theta, omega,
  sigma, SEs, OFV/AIC/BIC, diagnostics, model structure, warnings)
- `sdtab_to_dataframe()` — converts `Vec<SdtabRow>` into a data frame with
  columns PRED, IPRED, CWRES, IWRES, and one column per ETA

## Key types

### `Subject`

Holds all per-subject data after the NONMEM CSV is parsed.

```rust
pub struct Subject {
    pub id: String,
    pub doses: Vec<DoseEvent>,
    pub obs_times: Vec<f64>,
    pub observations: Vec<f64>,
    pub obs_cmts: Vec<usize>,
    pub covariates: HashMap<String, f64>,           // static (first non-missing)
    pub dose_covariates: Vec<HashMap<String, f64>>, // LOCF snapshot per dose event
    pub obs_covariates: Vec<HashMap<String, f64>>,  // LOCF snapshot per observation
    pub cens: Vec<u8>,       // 0 = observed, 1 = BLOQ (LLOQ stored in observations)
    pub occasions: Vec<u32>, // IOV occasion index per observation; empty if no IOV
    // ...
}
```

When a subject has no time-varying covariates, `dose_covariates` and
`obs_covariates` are empty and callers fall through to the static `covariates`
map. Methods `obs_cov(j)` / `dose_cov(k)` encapsulate this fallback.

### `DoseEvent`

```rust
pub struct DoseEvent {
    pub time: f64,
    pub amt: f64,
    pub cmt: usize,
    pub rate: f64,
    pub duration: f64,  // derived: amt / rate when rate > 0, else 0
    pub ss: bool,
    pub ii: f64,
}
```

### `PkParams`

Fixed-size array instead of `HashMap<String, f64>` — required for Enzyme AD.

```rust
pub struct PkParams {
    pub values: [f64; 8],  // MAX_PK_PARAMS = 8
}
// Index:  0=CL  1=V  2=Q  3=V2  4=KA  5=F  6=Q3  7=V3
```

`F` defaults to 1.0. Unused slots default to 0.0.

### `ModelParameters`

The full set of population parameters under estimation.

```rust
pub struct ModelParameters {
    pub theta: Vec<f64>,
    pub theta_names: Vec<String>,
    pub theta_lower: Vec<f64>,
    pub theta_upper: Vec<f64>,
    pub theta_fixed: Vec<bool>,
    pub omega: OmegaMatrix,  // caches Cholesky factor + free_mask
    pub sigma: SigmaVector,
    // ...
}
```

`OmegaMatrix` caches the Cholesky factor alongside the full matrix and carries
a `free_mask` (`DMatrix<bool>`) that records which off-diagonal entries are
free parameters versus structural zeros from `block_omega` declarations.

## Internal parameterisation

The outer optimizer works on an unconstrained packed vector `x`:

```
x = [log(theta_free), chol(Omega_BSV), log(sigma), chol(Omega_IOV)]
```

- **Theta**: log-transformed to enforce positivity; unpacked with `exp()`
- **Omega**: lower Cholesky factor; diagonal elements log-transformed;
  unpacked with `L * L'`
- **Sigma**: log-transformed, same as theta

This ensures the optimizer never visits invalid parameter regions (negative
variances, non-positive-definite omega). Parameter scaling (`scale_params =
true`) additionally normalizes the packed vector to O(1) before each optimizer
call to improve conditioning when parameters span several orders of magnitude.

## Parallelism

The outer optimizer evaluates subjects in parallel via
[rayon](https://docs.rs/rayon):

```rust
let subject_nlls: Vec<f64> = population
    .subjects
    .par_iter()
    .map(|subject| foce_subject_nll(subject, &params, &model))
    .collect();
```

The inner optimizer (EBE search) is per-subject and single-threaded; no nested
parallelism is used. Thread count is controlled by `FitOptions::n_threads`,
forwarded to rayon's global thread pool at the start of each fit call.

## Source tree

```
src/
├── api.rs                     public entry points
├── types.rs                   Subject, DoseEvent, PkParams, ModelParameters, FitResult
├── lib.rs                     crate root
├── build_info.rs              compile-time build metadata
├── cancel.rs                  cooperative cancellation token
├── parser/
│   └── model_parser.rs        .ferx DSL → CompiledModel
├── io/
│   ├── datareader.rs          read_nonmem_csv → Population
│   └── output.rs              sdtab / YAML writer
├── estimation/
│   ├── outer_optimizer.rs     population-level loop, NLopt/BFGS dispatch
│   ├── inner_optimizer.rs     per-subject EBE, gradient resolution (AD/FD)
│   ├── saem.rs                SAEM with MH sampling, two-phase schedule
│   ├── gauss_newton.rs        Gauss-Newton / BHHH with LM damping
│   ├── trust_region.rs        Newton trust-region (Steihaug CG subproblem)
│   ├── parameterization.rs    mu-referencing, log/logit transforms, pack/unpack
│   ├── sir.rs                 SIR importance resampling
│   └── trace.rs               iteration trace CSV writer
├── pk/
│   ├── one_compartment.rs     1-cpt IV bolus/infusion/oral analytical solutions
│   ├── two_compartment.rs     2-cpt analytical solutions, Vieta macro-rate constants
│   ├── three_compartment.rs   3-cpt IV bolus/infusion, Cardano characteristic roots
│   └── event_driven.rs        multi-dose superposition, TV-covariate dispatch
├── ode/
│   ├── solver.rs              RK45 Dormand-Prince adaptive integrator
│   └── predictions.rs         event handling, break-time construction
├── stats/
│   ├── likelihood.rs          FOCE/FOCEI NLL, CWRES, population objective
│   ├── residual_error.rs      IWRES, residual variance by error model type
│   └── special.rs             log-gamma, logit, log-normal CDF
├── ad/
│   ├── dual.rs                Dual<f64> forward-mode number type
│   ├── ad_gradients.rs        Enzyme reverse-mode gradient entry points
│   ├── event_driven_ad.rs     AD-compatible event-driven propagator
│   └── event_driven_ad_jac.rs Jacobian accumulation for Enzyme reverse mode
└── bin/
    ├── run_model.rs           CLI entry point
    └── generate_data.rs       synthetic data generator
```
